Subscribers onlyEgypt outlines a vast privatization plan  Weakened by the consequences of the war in Ukraine, the country has been forced to negotiate a new loan from the International Monetary Fund and wants to double the private sector's share in its economy.

Subscribers onlyJoe Biden reaffirms US commitment to defend Taiwan in the event of Chinese aggression  During his visit to Tokyo, the American president showed his willingness to stand up to Beijing's ambitions and to strengthen United States ties with allied countries in the region.
French court confirms it will drop rape case against film director Luc Besson  Actress Sand Van Roy had accused Luc Besson of raping her. Her lawyer says that she will appeal the court's decision.

Letter from...   Louis Imbert Subscribers onlyA Ukrainian woman takes refuge in Gaza to escape the war Viktoria Saidam and her husband have taken refuge in one of the most closed-off places on the planet, where 2.3 million Palestinians have been living under an Israeli-Egyptian blockade since 2007.   4 min read

Column   Léo Bourdin Subscribers onlyFrom chia seeds to raclette – served in a bowl, of course A simple way to make a dish attractive and create the impression that it's 'healthy' is to serve it in a bowl. But with many types of food presented this way, the round container has ended up becoming the unlikely Trojan horse of junk food.   4 min read

Your stories   Valentin Pérez Reda Kateb: 'Acting is not far off from Gnaoua music. It's a ritual. You are possessed by characters and stories' The French actor is the hero of 'Nos Frangins,' a film about the French-Algerian student Malik Oussekine killed by the police in 1986 that premiered at Cannes. He looks back on a night spent with Gnaoua musicians in Morocco 20 years ago.   2 min read
